overview.whyItMatters: AI governance ensures your GenAI systems are auditable, compliant, and accountable. As AI regulations evolve, having proper governance frameworks in place protects your organization. This isn't just about avoiding penalties—it's about building trustworthy AI.

concepts.title: Model Cards and Documentation
concepts.content:
Model cards are standardized documentation for AI models. They describe capabilities, limitations, intended uses, and ethical considerations.

**SageMaker Model Cards:**
Programmatically create and manage model documentation. Include:
- Model purpose and intended use cases
- Training data descriptions
- Performance metrics across different conditions
- Known limitations and failure modes
- Ethical considerations and potential biases
- Recommended use guidelines

**Why Model Cards Matter:**
- Transparency: Users understand what the model can and cannot do
- Accountability: Clear record of model capabilities at deployment
- Compliance: Documentation required by emerging AI regulations
- Knowledge transfer: New team members understand model context

**Model Registry:**
SageMaker Model Registry stores model versions with metadata. Track which model version is deployed where. Roll back to previous versions if issues arise. Model cards attach to specific versions.

**Integration with Governance:**
Model cards feed governance processes. Compliance teams review cards before deployment approval. Updates to cards trigger review workflows. Audit trails show model evolution over time.
keyPoints: Model cards document capabilities, limitations, intended use
SageMaker Model Cards for programmatic documentation
Model Registry tracks versions with attached documentation
Cards support compliance, transparency, and accountability
concepts.examTip: 'Document model capabilities' or 'model transparency' -> SageMaker Model Cards.

concepts.title: Data Lineage and Source Tracking
concepts.content:
Understanding where data comes from, how it transforms, and where it goes is essential for governance. Data lineage tracks this journey.

**AWS Glue Data Lineage:**
Automatically tracks data as it flows through Glue ETL jobs. Visualize transformations from source to destination. Understand impact of source changes on downstream systems.

**Glue Data Catalog:**
Central metadata repository. Register data sources with descriptions, schemas, and classifications. Tag datasets with sensitivity levels, ownership, and compliance requirements. The catalog is your source of truth for available data.

**Metadata Tagging for FM Outputs:**
Tag generated content with source attribution. When FM generates from Knowledge Base documents, track which sources contributed. Enable users to verify and trace claims.

**Lineage Use Cases:**
- Compliance audits: Show where data came from
- Impact analysis: Understand downstream effects of source changes
- Debugging: Trace issues to source data problems
- Attribution: Credit content sources appropriately
keyPoints: Glue Data Lineage tracks data transformation flows
Data Catalog centralizes metadata with classifications
Tag FM outputs with source attribution
Lineage supports audits, impact analysis, debugging

concepts.title: Audit Logging and Compliance Tracking
concepts.content:
Every significant action in your GenAI system should be logged for audit purposes. This creates an unmodifiable record of what happened, when, and by whom.

**CloudTrail for API Calls:**
CloudTrail logs all AWS API calls automatically. Every Bedrock invocation, every SageMaker endpoint call, every S3 access. Who made the call, when, from where, what parameters.

Enable CloudTrail across all regions. Configure log file validation to detect tampering. Store logs in locked-down S3 buckets with object lock.

**CloudWatch Logs for Decision Logs:**
Log application-level decisions that CloudTrail doesn't capture. What prompts were used, what guardrails triggered, what outputs were generated. These operational logs complement CloudTrail's API-level logs.

**Compliance Requirements:**
Different regulations require different logging:
- GDPR: Data access and processing logs
- HIPAA: PHI access logs
- SOC 2: Security event logs
- Industry-specific: Financial, healthcare, government

**Log Retention:**
Retain logs according to compliance requirements. S3 Lifecycle policies manage retention. Some regulations require multi-year retention. Balance cost with compliance needs.
keyPoints: CloudTrail logs all AWS API calls automatically
CloudWatch Logs for application-level decision logging

concepts.title: Governance Systems and Continuous Monitoring
concepts.content:
Governance is ongoing, not one-time. Systems must continuously monitor for policy violations, drift, and misuse.

**AWS Organizations:**
Apply policies across accounts. Service Control Policies (SCPs) enforce guardrails at the organization level. Prevent accounts from disabling required controls. Centralize governance for multi-account GenAI deployments.

**AWS Config:**
Continuously evaluate resource configurations against rules. Detect non-compliant configurations: endpoints without encryption, buckets without access logging, roles with excessive permissions. Auto-remediate or alert.

**Continuous Monitoring:**
- Misuse detection: Unusual usage patterns, policy violations
- Drift detection: Model output quality degrading over time
- Policy violation alerts: Guardrails triggering frequently
- Bias monitoring: Output patterns showing systematic bias

**CloudWatch for GenAI Monitoring:**
Custom metrics for GenAI-specific monitoring. Track guardrail trigger rates, output quality scores, latency distributions. Set alarms for anomalies. Dashboard visibility for governance teams.

**Responsible AI Framework:**
Beyond technical controls, establish organizational frameworks:
- Ethics review for new GenAI use cases
- Bias testing requirements before deployment
- Human oversight for high-risk applications
- Incident response procedures for AI issues
keyPoints: Organizations + SCPs enforce governance across accounts
Continuous monitoring for misuse, drift, violations, bias
CloudWatch metrics and dashboards for GenAI governance

skillExplanations.explanation:
Compliance frameworks require documentation, tracking, and controls. SageMaker Model Cards provide standardized model documentation. Glue Data Lineage tracks data flow. Metadata tagging enables attribution and traceability.

Build compliance into your architecture from the start. Retrofit is expensive. Design for auditability: log everything, tag everything, document everything. Make compliance automatic, not manual effort.

awsServices.whenToUse: Model Cards for documentation. Model Registry for version tracking.
Data lineage tracking. Data Catalog for metadata management.
Metrics and dashboards for compliance monitoring.

skillExplanations.explanation:
Data source tracking enables attribution and audit. Glue Data Catalog registers data sources with metadata. Tag sources with classification, ownership, and sensitivity. Use this metadata throughout the pipeline.

CloudTrail logs API-level access. Every Bedrock call, every S3 read. This creates an immutable audit trail. Enable log file validation to detect tampering.

Source attribution in outputs connects generated content to sources. When RAG retrieves documents, track which sources contributed. Users can verify claims by checking sources.

awsServices.whenToUse: Register and classify data sources. Central metadata repository.
Audit logging for all API calls. Immutable record.

skillExplanations.explanation:
Governance systems enforce organizational policies. AWS Organizations with SCPs apply guardrails across accounts. Prevent disabling of required controls. Centralize governance for GenAI across the organization.

AWS Config continuously evaluates configurations. Rules detect non-compliant resources. Auto-remediation fixes issues automatically. Compliance dashboards show organizational posture.

Responsible AI requires more than technical controls. Ethics review, bias testing, human oversight, incident response. Build organizational processes alongside technical systems.

awsServices.whenToUse: Organizational policies via SCPs. Multi-account governance.

skillExplanations.explanation:
Continuous monitoring detects issues before they become incidents. Misuse detection looks for unusual patterns: unexpected users, abnormal volumes, policy violations. Drift detection tracks output quality over time.

Bias monitoring examines output patterns for systematic unfairness. Are certain groups getting different treatment? CloudWatch metrics can track bias indicators. Bedrock Model Evaluations can test for bias systematically.

Alerts notify teams of issues. Don't rely on manual review of logs. Automate detection and alerting. CloudWatch Alarms trigger on metric thresholds. SNS delivers notifications to response teams.

awsServices.whenToUse: Metrics, alarms, dashboards for GenAI monitoring.
Model Evaluations for systematic bias testing.

values.CloudTrail: API calls, who did what when

examStrategies.approach: SageMaker Model Cards for standardized model documentation. Attach to Model Registry versions for tracking.
Glue Data Lineage for transformation tracking. Data Catalog for source registration. CloudTrail for access logging.
AWS Organizations with SCPs for multi-account policy. AWS Config for continuous compliance evaluation.

keyTakeaways: Model Cards document capabilities, limitations, and intended use
Glue Data Catalog + Lineage track data sources and transformations
CloudTrail logs all API calls; CloudWatch Logs for application decisions
Organizations + SCPs enforce governance across accounts
Continuous monitoring for misuse, drift, violations, and bias
commonMistakes: Not knowing where training/context data comes from
Disabling CloudTrail logging to save costs
Manual compliance checks instead of automated Config rules
No monitoring for model drift or bias after deployment
